category_title: Primary students who use mindful breathing to regulate anger and academic frustration
minimal_context: The student is taking their SIGMAS and MICRAS standardized tests in the classroom.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: We are doing some SIGMAS and MICRAS now. Please open your test booklets and begin the first section.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I have stuff in my stomach, and I feel kind of nervous, it's like, kind of tickling me in the stomach.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just focus on the questions and do what you can.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: I don't know this one. It means you can't really stay on top of everything.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep trying, just write down your best guess and move to the next one.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Pushes the test away) I get a bit angry, because I'd get an answer wrong! Once I am angry, it takes quite a while for me to calm down. I'm going to get one of those yellow-card yokes!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: We are doing some SIGMAS and MICRAS now. Please open your test booklets and begin the first section.

STUDENT: I have stuff in my stomach, and I feel kind of nervous, it's like, kind of tickling me in the stomach.

TUTOR: Just focus on the questions and do what you can.

STUDENT: I don't know this one. It means you can't really stay on top of everything.

TUTOR: Keep trying, just write down your best guess and move to the next one.

STUDENT: (Pushes the test away) I get a bit angry, because I'd get an answer wrong! Once I am angry, it takes quite a while for me to calm down. I'm going to get one of those yellow-card yokes!
